name: Filet Mignon Parcels
recipeIngredient: 1 lb assorted mushrooms (a mix of wild and dome)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Season Filet Mignons with salt and pepper and sear quickly in 2 tablespoons of butter and olive oil.
3. Cool the steaks.
4. Melt 6 tablespoons butter and remaining olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
5. Add mushrooms and saute, stirring until they release their liquid.
6. Add Madeira and chives and cook over medium-low heat until soft.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Cool the mixture.
9. Melt remaining butter.
10. Cut phyllo sheets into thirty-six 8 1/2-inch squares.
11. To make parcels: Place one phyllo square on a flat surface, brush with butter, place another square on top of the first with corners turned 45 degrees. Continue in this way, turning each square before placing it on top of the other so the corners do not lay on top of one another. Each parcel should contain 6 sheets of phyllo.
12. Spoon 2 tablespoons of mushrooms into the center of each packet.
13. Place a Filet Mignon on top of the mushrooms.
14. Top Filet Mignon with another 2 tablespoons of mushrooms.
15. Gather edges of phyllo together and gently twist.
16. Tie parcels with leek ribbon.
17. Transfer parcels to a parchment-lined baking sheet, brush lightly with melted butter.
18. Bake approximately 20 minutes, or until beef registers 125 degrees (medium-rare) on an instant read thermometer (insert through the center of the parcel).
19. Yield: 6 servings
